title: copier: add GetOptions.NoCrossDevice
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind feature

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Add a `NoCrossDevice` flag to `copier.GetOptions`, telling it to ignore subdirectories on devices different than the top reference directory that we start from, i.e., ignore the contents of mounted filesystems.

#### How to verify it

New unit test!

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

Not _completely_ sure if the home device of the directory passed as the second parameter to Get() is the right thing to compare to, but if we used the top of a directory tree as the baseline when descending into directories, if the top directory was a mountpoint (e.g., if we were copying "*" from "/", and the glob matched "/proc"), the flag wouldn't end up changing the archive we produced.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```
None
```

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [nalind] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Buildah CLI not using this option?

`COPY` doesn't have a switch for it, so this doesn't add one to the CLI.  Better to wait until we're sure that the way it works is the way we want it to work.  The whole "copying a directory copies its contents" thing we have to do for compatibility's sake makes the rule confusing no matter what.

CI couldn't pull the registry image from DockerHub.  Perhaps a rate limit, but it doesn't have the right foot print for that.  Retrying.
Otherwise,
LGTM
